output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here’s How to Get It Back Fast
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, what landlords can deduct, and how to demand your deposit back in 24 hours.
content_outline: Why Your Landlord Is Withholding Your Security Deposit (And Why They Might Be Wrong)
What a Landlord Can and Cannot Deduct From Your Deposit
Tenant Rights: How Long Does a Landlord Have to Return Your Deposit?
What to Do When Your Landlord Won’t Return Your Deposit
How to Write a Security Deposit Dispute Letter That Actually Works
Get Your Deposit Back: The 24-Hour Demand Letter Solution
key_points: Landlords must return security deposits within a state-specific timeframe—usually 14 to 60 days—and provide an itemized list of any deductions.
Normal wear and tear cannot be deducted; landlords may only withhold funds for actual damage beyond ordinary use or unpaid rent.
A formal, state-compliant demand letter is often the fastest and most effective way to recover a withheld deposit before escalating to small claims court.
DepositBack enables tenants to generate legally sound security deposit demand letters in minutes, creating immediate pressure for landlords to pay.
output.cta: Don’t let your landlord keep what’s yours. Generate your state-specific security deposit demand letter with DepositBack today and recover your money fast.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters whose landlords are withholding, delaying, or refusing to return a security deposit and need immediate, legally sound help to recover their money.
